description: This scatter chart compares the specific energy absorption (E_spec, J/mm³) of Fe-Mn-Al-C steels against conventional deep-drawing steels (IF steel, HC300LA, and DC04) as a function of aluminum content (wt%) at a crash-relevant deformation rate of 10³/s. The conventional steels, indicated by open symbols in the legend, exhibit low E_spec values between approximately 0.16 and 0.25 J/mm³. In contrast, Fe-Mn-Al-C steels (green diamonds) show strongly elevated energy absorption: at ~6.3 wt% Al, values scatter between ~0.27 and 0.34 J/mm³, while at Al contents above 7 wt%, E_spec stabilizes around ~0.5 J/mm³ (ranging from ~0.43 to 0.56 J/mm³), comparable to Mn TWIP steels and roughly 50% higher than conventional grades. The caption and surrounding text attribute this enhanced energy absorption to severe shear band formation at high strain rates, specifically adiabatic shear band (ASB) formation, where localized deformation creates momentary high temperatures that cannot be readily dissipated in these low-thermal-conductivity alloys.
